findings.message: Adding "email" to frontend_rum_allowed_query_params collects the user's email address from the URL query string into RUM/telemetry. Email is PII, and capturing it into attribution telemetry is collection of PII not needed for the stated purpose (query-string attribution is for UTM campaign params, not personal identifiers). Once ingested it is retained in telemetry beyond any attribution purpose.
findings.suggestion: Remove the "email" entry from the allowlist. Keep only non-PII attribution keys (the utm_* set). If per-user correlation is genuinely required, use a pseudonymous, server-issued identifier rather than capturing raw email, and never source it from the untrusted browser query string.
